column1: # Graph offensive v2 — Spark-feasible subset after negative CUDA-graph perf result.
# Finding: on GB10, mode=reduce-overhead gives <0.2% delta vs default (noise floor).
# max-autotune crashes ptxas (sm_121a codegen bug). Therefore the relevant questions
# are NOT "which mode is fastest on Spark" but:
#   Q1: Does compile=default even beat eager? (All prior Spark runs were eager.)
#   Q2: Is reduce-overhead correctness-preserving through the full real training
#       loop (layer-loop toggle, TTT, EMA, GPTQ pipeline)? Needed before blind-bet
# 3 rows, 500 iters, same seed. Interleaved so machine drift is bounded.

column2: QK_GAIN_INIT=5.5 WARMDOWN_FRAC=0.64 TTT_ENABLED=1 SLIDING_WINDOW_ENABLED=1 TTT_EPOCHS=1 EMA_DECAY=0.995 LOGIT_SOFTCAP=20 MATRIX_LR=0.042 ITERATIONS=500 MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=1800 TIMEOUT_SECS=3600 FAST_SMOKE=0 SEED=42 TORCHDYNAMO_DISABLE=1 TORCH_COMPILE_DISABLE=1 NOTES=eager_control
